creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: 6 Things to Know about AIE World's Fair 2026
videos.summary: The AI Engineer channel promotes AIE World's Fair 2026 in San Francisco, highlighting expo floor experiences, research-industry synergy, leadership tracks, and AI vertical focus. Attendees can claim $40k in sponsor offers and use discount code YOUTUBEPROMO.

videos.title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
videos.summary: Sandipan Bhaumik from Databricks presents five pillars for deploying agents at enterprise scale: evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance. He illustrates the framework through a case study where a retail bank's failed chatbot was salvaged by investing six weeks in tracing and measurement infrastructure before model selection, and later caught a critical production bug through observability when policy documents became stale.

videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: Agents often claim to have searched the web but fail to do so when blocked by anti-bot systems, CAPTCHAs, or stale pages, then hallucinate answers. Rafael Levi demonstrates using Bright Data's Web MCP to provide agents with genuine web access, comparing failures across LinkedIn, Instagram, Amazon, and TikTok to show how real access reduces hallucinations and improves citation reliability.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: NVIDIA's team achieved real-time video diffusion generation on Blackwell B200 by reducing denoising steps from 50 to as few as 1-4 through quantization, latent caching, and step distillation. The approach is modular with additive speedups that can reach 10-200x acceleration, packaged in the open source FastGen repository.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: AI engineers use automated loops that prompt agents repeatedly instead of manual prompting. This approach has agents prompting agents, with orchestrators spinning up parallel workers. The pattern reduces human friction but increases computational cost and hallucination risk.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness framework that allows multiple coding agents from different providers to work together as interchangeable workers. It supports Claude Code, Codex, and other models, enabling task orchestration with governance layers and cross-device session continuity.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin reports that Claude Fable 5, Anthropic's newest model, was pulled from availability three days after launch following a US government export-control directive over alleged jailbreak vulnerabilities. The stream covers the regulatory shutdown, its implications for model builders, and Medin's benchmarks comparing Fable 5 to Opus 4.8.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that enables rapid development and production deployment of AI agents. It provides agent identity, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails, simplifying the process of building reliable AI agent systems.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses Loop Engineer, a technique for setting up and building AI agents. The video covers the fundamentals and provides practical setup instructions with associated resources including GitHub templates and workshop materials.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: AI Jason explains how to construct proactive agents and self-improving company systems using AI loops. The video covers architectural patterns for autonomous agents, demonstrates use cases in ads optimization and SEO, and highlights tools like Loopany and memory systems that enable these capabilities.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and its autonomous coding capabilities, comparing it to Ralph-loop 2.0. The video covers setup, prompting techniques, and mission exploration for long-form task execution, positioning it as a step toward true autonomous coding.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses OpenAI Symphony as a new coding paradigm. The video covers how Symphony works, its harness setup and auto-testing capabilities, and configuration via WORKFLOW.md.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains Andrej Karpathy's Bigram Language Model in a 10-minute video tutorial. The content covers tokenization, embedding, training, loss calculation, and backpropagation as foundational concepts for understanding larger language models like GPT.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 series comprising Nano, Super, and Ultra variants. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer architecture, Latent MoE, and MTP techniques to differentiate from other open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model featuring MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) that replaces full attention with sparse attention mechanisms. The model includes tiled optimizations and I/O improvements for KV cache reading, reducing computational cost in prefill and decode stages.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains Pi, an agent framework that enables flexible harness layers for AI applications. The video compares Pi against frontier coding agents like Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex, examining how the tool handles increasing complexity in product features.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS is a framework that equips AI coding agents with up-to-date AWS documentation, best practices, and tested procedures. It reduces reliance on agents' general knowledge and improves success rates when working with AWS services.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers walkthrough on deploying Claude Code via Amazon Bedrock for data residency and compliance. Covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and Guardrails for content governance.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses job hunting strategies in an AI-driven market, emphasizing networking, personal branding, and the evolving but persistent role of junior developers. The video argues that AI will transform job categories rather than eliminate them, using historical parallels like transportation industry disruption.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents five production deployment patterns for AI agents: streaming tool call events for responsive UIs, layered authentication using API Gateway and Cognito with IAM bridges, type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails and cost budgets with model routing, and observability via metrics and distributed tracing. The patterns apply across platforms and are demonstrated using Strands Agents SDK on AWS infrastructure.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses whether Google engineers use AI-assisted code generation. The video compares OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities, examines Google's internal coding practices in 2024-2026, and addresses what qualifies as "vibe coding."

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or project positioned as disruptive to open source software. The video examines tensions between open source maintainers and commercial interests, with commentary on whether Malus represents an ethical problem for the open source community.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that software moats depend less on code itself and more on customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational complexity. He contends that AI will not eliminate SaaS companies as feared, and emphasizes that the best AI products create devoted users through customer obsession and design excellence.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a ChatGPT-like moment is coming for robotics, but humanoid design focus may be myopic. She advocates for prioritizing human interaction and delight in robot design over narrow utility, shaping how billions of robots will integrate into society.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how a Paris-based AI performance management startup competes against much larger enterprises by building a full platform from day one rather than single features. She shares insights on enterprise sales strategy, hiring philosophy, and how companies like Google use spreadsheets for strategic planning despite having advanced technology capabilities.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that real product-market fit is signaled by customer pull (pricing inquiries) rather than passion, and that founders should embed in customer workflows instead of running formal interviews. He warns against mistaking a small passionate segment for true PMF and advises betting on AI model trajectories rather than current capabilities.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Location Control: Give Clients FULL Power! #shorts
videos.summary: A short-form video argues that clients should have full control over AI infrastructure operations and geographical deployment locations. The content emphasizes the importance of transparency and client power in dictating where and how artificial intelligence systems operate.

videos.title: Anthropic's AI Future: GPUs, Power & Unseen Security! #shorts
videos.summary: A YouTube short discussing Anthropic's AI infrastructure, focusing on GPU costs, power requirements, and security mechanisms like sandboxes and MCP tunnels. The video explores the operational infrastructure needed to support modern AI systems.

videos.title: AI Agents: DEAD or ALIVE? Businesses Are Still Waiting! #shorts
videos.summary: Joao argues that advanced AI agents with sophisticated capabilities already exist today. However, businesses remain hesitant and slow to adopt these tools in practice, creating a gap between technological availability and enterprise deployment.

videos.title: AI Adoption Gap: Businesses Are SLOWLY Catching Up! #shorts
videos.summary: Advanced AI capabilities are developing faster than businesses can adopt them, with agentic AI seeing particularly slow enterprise uptake. This adoption gap represents missed opportunities for companies seeking transformative growth.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
